reasoning: All models skipped the critical PKGBUILD file due to incorrect binary file detection, preventing any meaningful security analysis. Without reviewing PKGBUILD, no determination of safety can be made.
coverage_issues: All models failed to review PKGBUILD – the core file defining build/install steps and potential risks
Critical files like install hooks or referenced scripts weren't reviewed due to initial PKGBUILD skip
false_negatives: Potential risks in PKGBUILD (malicious commands, unsafe permissions) were completely missed by all models
Any referenced scripts or hooks in the package were never examined
learnings: Fix binary file detection: PKGBUILD is always text; tool must not misclassify core build scripts
Prioritization failure: Models should abort with error if PKGBUILD can't be read rather than skipping
Add fallback mechanism: When filetype detection fails, attempt safe text decoding for critical files
Improve pre-audit checks: Validate file readability before model invocation to avoid wasted audits
